attacking_style: Not a traditional attacker. Opportunistic sprinter who capitalizes on chaos in reduced bunch sprints, showing ability to finish strongly from 200-250m when positioned well without formal leadout.
team_tactics: Initially deployed as leadout man but demonstrates capacity to freelance when team sprint trains collapse. Benefits from general team pace-forcing that fragments sprints rather than controlled leadouts.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages and chaotic finishes where positioning matters more than pure top-end speed. Completely exposed on any sustained climbing - drops quickly on mountain stages beyond 50km from finish.
vulnerability: Mountain stages eliminate him decisively (2+ minute losses on 50km climbs). Pure sprint trains with organized leadouts may disadvantage him versus relying on instinctive positioning in disrupted finishes.
key_patterns: Consistently places top-5 in sprints across multiple Grand Tours when positioned correctly (2021, 2022)
Loses significant time on mountain stages, 2+ minutes when climbs extend beyond 50km
Performs better in disrupted/chaotic sprints than organized trains with traditional leadouts (2019, 2021)
Values consistent high placings and bonus seconds over risky stage win attempts (2022 Vuelta)
form_trajectory: positive: evolved from pure leadout man (2019) to consistent top-5 finisher with improved tactical awareness in sprint chaos (2021-2022)
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 5
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 2
